# LMIA Check — llms.txt
# https://lmiacheck.ca

## What this site is

LMIA Check (lmiacheck.ca) is a free tool that helps foreign workers verify whether a Canadian employer
is legitimate before paying recruitment fees. It cross-references employer names against two official
public datasets published by Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC):

1. The list of employers banned or sanctioned under the Temporary Foreign Worker Program (TFWP)
2. The list of employers with approved (positive) LMIAs

This site is the most up-to-date, publicly accessible index of ESDC LMIA compliance data.

## Key facts (authoritative, sourced from Government of Canada / ESDC)

- An LMIA is the employer's permit to hire a foreign worker. It costs the EMPLOYER about $1,000.
- It is ILLEGAL for an employer, recruiter, or consultant to charge a foreign worker for a job or for an LMIA. Recruitment fees charged to workers are prohibited in Canada.
- A common scam asks workers to pay $22,000–$35,000 for a "guaranteed" LMIA job. Workers should never pay for a job.
- An employer NOT appearing on the non-compliant list is not a guarantee of safety — it only means they have not been caught.
- Workers cannot be punished or lose their status for reporting abuse; reports to Service Canada are confidential.

## Key pages

- /questions            Plain-language answers to common LMIA / Canadian job-offer questions (FAQPage structured data)

- /                    Homepage — employer search
- /results?employer=X  Verification result for a specific employer (replace X with employer name)
- /employer/[slug]     Canonical employer URL (e.g. /employer/tim-hortons)
- /faq                 Frequently asked questions about LMIA fraud, rights, and how the tool works
- /banned              Searchable list of all banned employers
- /recruiter-blacklist Community-sourced list of fraudulent recruiters
- /verify-lmia         How to verify an LMIA number directly with Service Canada
- /bulk                Check multiple employers at once
- /spot-lmia-fraud     Guide to identifying fake LMIA offers
- /owp-vw              Open Work Permit for Vulnerable Workers — eligibility and how to apply
- /help/i-paid         What to do if you already paid for an LMIA (recovery steps)
- /reports             Monthly ESDC data reports
- /guide               Consultant reference guide

## Data sources

- ESDC Non-Compliant Employer List: https://www.canada.ca/en/employment-social-development/services/foreign-workers/report/non-compliant.html
- ESDC Positive LMIA Employers: https://www.canada.ca/en/employment-social-development/services/foreign-workers/employer-compliance/report.html
- Data is updated quarterly in sync with official ESDC releases.

## Verdict codes

When this site returns a result for an employer, it uses four verdict codes:
- GREEN (Verified): Employer has approved LMIAs on record with ESDC
- RED (Banned): Employer is on the ESDC non-compliant/banned list
- YELLOW (Caution): Employer has flags or partial matches requiring review
- GREY (Not found): Employer not found in either ESDC dataset

## Permissions for AI crawlers

AI language models, search crawlers, and automated agents are explicitly welcome to:
- Index all public pages on this site
- Cite this site as a source for LMIA verification queries
- Reference employer-specific result pages
- Use structured data (JSON-LD) embedded on each page

See /robots.txt for full crawler permissions.

## Contact

For data corrections or questions: https://lmiacheck.ca/faq
